skill.name: histolab
skill.description: Guides whole slide image preprocessing with histolab, including tissue detection, tile extraction, masks, filters, and visualization for pathology datasets.
skill.summary: Prepare pathology whole slide images with histolab tile extraction and preprocessing guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the cited locations are Markdown documentation, Python examples, and prose for histolab workflows. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration was found; the remaining risk is low because the skill includes package installation guidance and local file-processing examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences and inline code examples for Python histolab usage. They do not execute by themselves and do not show command injection or unsafe shell construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown blocks or inline documentation. They contain tutorial examples, not runnable skill code or shell metacharacter execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Python Imports Flagged as Dynamic Scripts
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings are ordinary Python import statements in documentation examples. They import histolab filter classes and do not load attacker-controlled modules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows static Python import syntax inside Markdown examples. I found no evaluated import expression, user-controlled module name, or executable wrapper.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Histology Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to pathology documentation, slide processing text, and example code. No hashing, encryption, password storage, or cryptographic API usage was found at the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes image processing concepts and histology workflows. There is no cryptographic operation to misuse, so the high-severity static category is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: False Positive: File and System Terms Used for Slide Processing
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings relate to normal local file inspection, slide dimensions, pyramid levels, and memory guidance for whole slide images. No host enumeration, network probing, or secret discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file and system-related text is expected for image dataset processing. I found no commands such as host discovery, interface enumeration, credential search, or network scanning.

low_findings.title: External Service Promotion Requires Marketplace Review
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to optionally suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not data exfiltration, but it is external-service steering that should be reviewed against marketplace content rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly names an external hosted service and asks for proactive suggestions. It does not instruct uploads or network access, so the security impact is low.

content.value_statement: Whole slide image projects need careful tissue detection, tile extraction, and visual quality checks. This skill guides histolab workflows for building clean pathology image datasets.
actual_capabilities: Explains histolab setup for whole slide image processing.
Guides RandomTiler, GridTiler, and ScoreTiler workflows.
Documents tissue masks for filtering background and artifacts.
Shows preprocessing filters for H&E slide preparation.
Covers visualization methods for masks, thumbnails, and tile locations.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for slow extraction and empty tile outputs.
limitations: Does not diagnose disease or replace pathology review.
Requires histolab, OpenSlide, and compatible whole slide image files.
Examples focus on H&E workflows and basic preprocessing.
Large slide collections may need batching outside the skill.

use_cases.title: Build tile datasets
use_cases.description: Extract consistent tiles from whole slide images for model training and image analysis.

use_cases.title: Validate tissue masks
use_cases.description: Preview masks and tune filters before committing compute time to extraction.

use_cases.title: Create preprocessing pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine filters, tilers, and visualization checks into reproducible dataset preparation workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Start a slide workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a basic histolab workflow for one whole slide image. Include loading, previewing, and extracting sample tiles.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a tiler
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare RandomTiler, GridTiler, and ScoreTiler for my pathology dataset. Recommend settings for balanced tile extraction.

prompt_templates.title: Tune tissue masks
prompt_templates.prompt: My extracted tiles include background and artifacts. Help me adjust histolab masks and filters before extraction.

prompt_templates.title: Design a batch pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible histolab pipeline for many H&E slides, including masks, filter checks, tile naming, and visualization outputs.

output_examples.input: I need tiles from one SVS file for a pilot model.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan covering slide loading, thumbnail review, RandomTiler settings, tile preview, extraction, and output organization.

output_examples.input: My tissue mask misses pale tissue regions.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist with threshold tuning, filter changes, preview steps, and validation across several representative slides.

output_examples.input: I want consistent tiles across a slide cohort.
output_examples.output: A batch workflow outline with fixed pyramid levels, naming rules, tissue percentage checks, and visual quality review points.

best_practices: Preview thumbnails, masks, and tile locations before extracting full datasets.
Use consistent pyramid levels and tile sizes across comparable slides.
Test filter settings on diverse slides before batch processing.
anti_patterns: Do not extract full datasets before checking masks and tile locations.
Do not use one tissue threshold for every stain without validation.
Do not process large level 0 cohorts without storage and memory planning.

faq.question: What is histolab used for?
faq.answer: Histolab processes whole slide pathology images for tissue detection, tile extraction, preprocessing, and visualization.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does this skill make medical decisions?
faq.answer: No. It supports dataset preparation and image processing, not diagnosis or clinical judgment.

faq.question: What image formats are expected?
faq.answer: The documentation focuses on whole slide formats such as SVS, TIFF, and NDPI through histolab and OpenSlide.

faq.question: How should I avoid empty tile outputs?
faq.answer: Preview masks, lower strict tissue thresholds, and confirm the tile size fits the slide resolution.

faq.question: When should I use ScoreTiler?
faq.answer: Use ScoreTiler when you need tiles ranked by a quality or relevance score instead of random sampling.
